questions: A new Mac user in Singapore is exploring official resources to both learn about their device and understand the purchasing terms if they buy directly from Apple’s online store in their country. Based on Apple’s publicly available information: (1) What are the key conditions of the standard return policy in Singapore, including the return window, product condition requirements, refund limitations, and any special rules for bulk purchases and price adjustments? (2) Through Apple’s main website navigation, which sections and specific support options would help this user access the Mac user guide and obtain technical assistance (such as manuals or in-person support)? Provide a structured explanation that integrates both the purchase policy details and the relevant navigation paths for Mac learning and support.

A new Mac user in Singapore wants to understand three different types of official Apple resources: (1) the regional online purchasing terms that apply when buying directly from Apple within Singapore, (2) the comprehensive navigation hub that organizes Apple’s products, services, retail options, and support links across its main website, and (3) the official macOS support hub that provides system recovery and maintenance guidance. 

Identify these three distinct resources and, for each one, provide specific details. For the Singapore purchasing terms, summarize the standard return window, the special conditions for bulk purchases (including any restocking fee and shortened return period), refund limitations related to bank accounts and geography, and the rules for price protection after a recent price drop. For the main website navigation hub, list several of its major top-level sections that cover shopping, support, business or education programs, and services. For the macOS support hub, describe at least three featured help topics related to account recovery, reinstalling the operating system, backups, or system updates, including how those processes are generally performed.

rounds.question: A new Mac user in Singapore is exploring official resources to both learn about their device and understand the purchasing terms if they buy directly from Apple’s online store in their country. Based on Apple’s publicly available information: (1) What are the key conditions of the standard return policy in Singapore, including the return window, product condition requirements, refund limitations, and any special rules for bulk purchases and price adjustments? (2) Through Apple’s main website navigation, which sections and specific support options would help this user access the Mac user guide and obtain technical assistance (such as manuals or in-person support)? Provide a structured explanation that integrates both the purchase policy details and the relevant navigation paths for Mac learning and support.
checklist_width: 14-day standard return window from date of receipt
Requirement that product be in original condition with included parts, accessories, and packaging
Refunds remitted only to a Singapore bank account matching the payor and not outside Singapore
Bulk purchase return rule: more than 4 units, 7-day return window, 25% restocking fee, return to original store
Price protection within 14 calendar days of receipt, request via contact center, limited to 10 units, excludes special promotions
Navigation path including Mac section under main menu (e.g., Mac → Support)
Access to Mac User Guide or Manuals via Support section
Reference to Genius Bar or in-person technical support option
checklist_depth: Singapore Apple Online Store Consumer Store policy identified as the applicable country-specific purchase and return terms
Apple website main navigation / Site Map structure identified as the source for Mac User Guide, Mac Support, Manuals, and Genius Bar options
rounds.rationale: The question uses the scenario of a new Mac user in Singapore (drawn from the Mac User Guide context) to logically filter for the Singapore-specific Apple Online Store policies (Deep reasoning). It then requires aggregation of multiple scattered policy elements (returns, bulk limits, refunds, price protection) and combines them with separate navigation and support information from Apple’s main site structure (Wide aggregation). The entities are masked behind functional descriptions rather than document names.
rounds.meta_context_snippet: [Wide Logic - Target 0]: Singapore Consumer Store Thanks for shopping at Apple. We appreciate the fact that you like to buy the cool stuff we build. We also want to make sure you have a rewarding expe
rounds.answer_a:
Based on the provided search results, specific return policy details for Singapore are not comprehensively available. However, the results indicate relevant support pathways:

**Return Policy Information:**
The search results do not contain explicit details about return windows, product condition requirements, refund limitations, or bulk purchase rules. Users should access Apple's official terms directly through their online store or contact support[2] for authoritative policy information.

**Mac Learning and Support Navigation:**

Users can access Mac resources through several official channels:

**Support Resources:** Apple Support Singapore[7] serves as the primary hub for product assistance. The Manuals, Specs, and Downloads section[6] specifically lists Mac documentation, enabling users to access user guides and technical specifications.

**Direct Assistance:** Users can reach Apple Support by phone or through the Apple Support app[2]. The app provides centralized help for all Apple products or connects users with experts. Phone support is available through location-specific numbers provided on Apple's contact page.

**In-Person Support:** The support page[2] mentions Genius Bar appointments for hands-on assistance with Mac issues and device setup.

**Additional Resources:** The Personal Safety User Guide resources[5] offers supplementary support materials, while the main Apple Singapore website[4] provides product overviews and specifications.

**Recommended Navigation Path:**
New Mac users should begin at support.apple.com/en-sg, access the Manuals section to download Mac user guides, then utilize the Apple Support app or phone support for technical assistance. For purchasing terms, checking the online order status page or contacting support directly ensures access to current, accurate policy information specific to Singapore.

## References

[2] https://support.apple.com/en-sg/contact
[4] https://www.apple.com/sg/
[5] https://support.apple.com/en-sg/guide/personal-safety/ips8f39ce6a8/web
[6] https://support.apple.com/en-sg/docs
[7] https://support.apple.com/en-sg
rounds.answer_b:
In Singapore, the standard return policy for the Apple online store allows returns within 14 calendar days from the delivery date. Products must be undamaged, in their original packaging, and include all accessories and documentation to qualify for a refund to the original payment method [1]. Customers may also request a price adjustment if the price drops within this 14-day window. However, special rules apply to bulk or corporate orders; these items are generally non-returnable once opened or activated unless defective, requiring specific authorization from Apple Sales [2].

To learn about the device, users should navigate to the “Support” tab on Apple’s website and select “Mac.” This section provides access to the official “Mac User Guide,” a comprehensive manual tailored to specific macOS versions [3]. For technical assistance, the “Contact Apple Support” page offers virtual help via chat or phone. Furthermore, users seeking in-person support can use the site to schedule “Genius Bar” appointments at local Apple Stores, where experts provide hands-on troubleshooting and hardware diagnostics. These integrated resources ensure new users can effectively master their device’s features while fully understanding their consumer rights regarding returns.

## References
[1] Apple, “Returns and Refunds,” Apple.com/sg, https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/returns_refund
[2] Apple, “Sales and Refunds,” Apple.com/sg, https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/sales_refunds
[3] Apple, “Mac Support,” Apple.com/sg, https://support.apple.com/mac
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Agent B correctly identifies the Apple Singapore Online Store returns pages and the Mac Support section as the relevant entities. Agent A fails to surface the Singapore Consumer Store return policy at all, instead deferring users to contact support, which does not satisfy the query. 

Second, Width/Completeness: Agent A omits nearly all required return policy details (14-day window, product condition, refund limitations, bulk purchase rules, price protection specifics). Agent B covers the 14-day return window, condition requirements, and price adjustment window, and references bulk rules (though inaccurately and without the specific 7-day/25% restocking details). B also properly explains navigation via Support → Mac, Mac User Guide, and Genius Bar. While B misses key refund limitations (e.g., Singapore bank account restriction) and misstates bulk conditions, it still addresses most checklist categories. A fails most of them.

Finally, User Experience & Structure: Agent B provides a clearer, more direct, and integrated explanation of both purchasing terms and support navigation. Agent A’s response is incomplete and deflective. Because Agent B contains inaccuracies in sub-points (bulk rules), the verdict is capped at BETTER rather than MUCH_BETTER.

lines: -> WINNER is B. Targeting Loser Failure: WIDE.

rounds.question:
A new Mac user in Singapore wants to understand three different types of official Apple resources: (1) the regional online purchasing terms that apply when buying directly from Apple within Singapore, (2) the comprehensive navigation hub that organizes Apple’s products, services, retail options, and support links across its main website, and (3) the official macOS support hub that provides system recovery and maintenance guidance. 

Identify these three distinct resources and, for each one, provide specific details. For the Singapore purchasing terms, summarize the standard return window, the special conditions for bulk purchases (including any restocking fee and shortened return period), refund limitations related to bank accounts and geography, and the rules for price protection after a recent price drop. For the main website navigation hub, list several of its major top-level sections that cover shopping, support, business or education programs, and services. For the macOS support hub, describe at least three featured help topics related to account recovery, reinstalling the operating system, backups, or system updates, including how those processes are generally performed.
checklist_width: Singapore store: 14-day standard return policy with receipt and original condition requirement
Singapore store: Bulk purchase return within 7 days plus 25% restocking fee
Singapore store: Refunds only to Singapore bank accounts matching payor and no refunds outside Singapore
Singapore store: 14-day price protection window (excluding special sales) and limit up to 10 units
Main navigation hub: Includes major sections such as Shop and Learn, Support, Services, and Today at Apple (or equivalent high-level categories)
macOS support hub: Reset Apple ID password guidance
macOS support hub: Reinstall macOS using macOS Recovery without removing personal data
macOS support hub: Back up Mac using Time Machine with an external storage device
checklist_depth: Singapore Consumer Store page (Apple Online Store Singapore Terms and Conditions) + proof via specific Singapore-only policies
Apple Site Map page + proof via broad top-level navigation categories across products, services, retail, and support
macOS Support page + proof via featured topics like password reset, macOS Recovery reinstall, and Time Machine backups
rounds.rationale: The question uses the Mac user journey (purchasing, navigating Apple’s ecosystem, and maintaining macOS) as a logical filter to indirectly point to three specific official Apple resources without naming them. It then requires aggregating multiple detailed policies and features from each resource, ensuring both deep identification (correct pages) and wide extraction (multiple scattered facts per page).

rounds.answer_a:
The provided search results do not contain comprehensive information about all three resources requested.

**Singapore Purchasing Terms:** The search results lack specific details about return windows, bulk purchase conditions, restocking fees, refund limitations, or price protection policies for Singapore's Apple online store. Users must access Apple's Singapore store directly or contact support for authoritative purchasing terms.[10]

**Main Website Navigation Hub:** While the search results reference Apple Support Singapore[1] as a resource hub, they do not detail the main Apple website's top-level navigation structure covering shopping, support, business programs, and services. Users should visit the primary Apple Singapore website to explore these sections, though specific navigation details are not provided in these results.

**macOS Support Hub:** The search results identify a dedicated Mac support resource but lack detailed documentation on specific help topics. However, available information indicates users can access:[1][2]

- **System information and specifications** through Apple menu > About This Mac and System Report
- **User guides and technical specifications** via the Apple Documentation website
- **Mac User Guide** covering macOS features, device setup, privacy, and security controls
- **Support resources** through the Mac Support website and Apple Support Community

The results confirm that comprehensive support materials exist but do not enumerate specific help topics related to account recovery, OS reinstallation, backups, or system updates procedures.

**Recommendation:** For complete information on all three resources, new Mac users in Singapore should directly visit support.apple.com/en-sg for support navigation[1][2] and the main Apple Singapore website for purchasing terms and primary navigation options. These official channels provide current, region-specific information that the current search results do not fully capture.

## References

[1] https://support.apple.com/en-sg/guide/mac-help/mchlf524065e/mac
[2] https://support.apple.com/en-sg/guide/mac-help/welcome/mac
[10] https://support.apple.com/en-sg/contact
rounds.answer_b:
The **Apple Online Store Singapore Terms & Conditions** govern regional purchases. The standard return window is 14 calendar days from delivery, requiring items to be undamaged [1]. For bulk orders, typically defined as five or more units of the same product, Apple may impose a shortened return period and potentially a restocking fee; these orders are often non-returnable once opened unless authorized [2]. Refund limitations stipulate that reimbursements are made to the original payment method, and while Apple processes refunds, the timing depends on the customer’s bank, with international restrictions potentially applying to cross-border accounts [1]. Price protection allows customers to request a refund for the difference if a price drop occurs within 14 days of purchase [2].

The **main Apple website navigation hub** serves as the central gateway. Its major top-level sections include “Store” for shopping, “Mac,” “iPad,” and “iPhone” for product details, and “Support” for technical help [3]. Additionally, the hub features sections dedicated to “Services” like Apple Music and iCloud, as well as specific portals for “Business” and “Education” programs, linking directly to relevant enterprise or academic solutions [3].

The **official macOS Support Hub** provides detailed system maintenance guidance. Featured help topics include account recovery, where users reset passwords using Apple ID or Recovery Key; system updates, performed via “System Settings” > “General” > “Software Update”; and data backup, managed through Time Machine [4]. For critical recovery, the hub guides users on reinstalling macOS by using macOS Recovery utilities, often accessed by holding down the power button or specific key combinations during startup to erase and reinstall the operating system [4].

## References
[1] Apple, “Returns and Refunds,” Apple.com/sg, https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/returns_refund
[2] Apple, “Sales and Refunds,” Apple.com/sg, https://www.apple.com/sg/shop/help/sales_refunds
[3] Apple, “Apple (Singapore),” Apple.com/sg, https://www.apple.com/sg
[4] Apple, “Mac Support,” Apple.com, https://support.apple.com/mac
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Deep Logic: Agent A failed to identify and describe the three required official resources (Singapore Terms & Conditions page, Apple main site navigation hub, and macOS Support hub). It largely refused and deferred users elsewhere. Agent B correctly identified all three core entities and grounded them in appropriate Apple Singapore and support pages. 

Width/Completeness: Agent A missed nearly all checklist items (no return window details, no bulk conditions, no refund/geography limits, no price protection rules, no meaningful macOS recovery topics). Agent B covered the 14-day return window, bulk purchase conditions, price protection window, major navigation sections (Store, Support, Services, Business, Education), and macOS help topics (password reset, Time Machine backups, macOS Recovery reinstall, system updates). However, B lacked some required specifics (e.g., 7-day bulk return + 25% restocking fee, Singapore-only bank account restriction, 10-unit price protection cap) and slightly generalized certain policies. 

User Experience & Presentation: Agent B provided structured, content-rich, well-organized information with useful procedural detail. Agent A delivered a sparse, unhelpful response with minimal actionable content.

Conclusion: Agent B clearly provides a far superior user experience and substantially better coverage, but due to some missing or imprecise sub-point details, the verdict is B_BETTER rather than MUCH_BETTER.
